title: Trask Ulgo

{{Quote|Now hurry up, we have to find Bastila! We have to make sure she makes it off the ship alive!|Trask Ulgo, to Revan|audio=TraskUlgo-FindBastila.flac|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
'''Trask Ulgo''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] into the [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[House Ulgo]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. By the height of the [[Jedi Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]], he had attained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Ensign/Legends|ensign]] with the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]]. During the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3956 BBY]], Ulgo was an [[Republic officer/Legends|officer]] stationed aboard the [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]] ''[[Endar Spire]]'', where he was the bunkmate of the [[Amnesia/Legends|amnesiac]] former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]. However, Ulgo believed that Revan was simply a new recruit, and since the two worked opposite shifts during normal duty, they did not interact with each other.

That year, their [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] fell under [[Attack on the Endar Spire|attack]] from the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Empire over the planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], due to the presence of the ship's [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]], [[Bastila Shan]]. Shortly after the attack began, Ulgo enlisted Revan's aid in finding Shan both to help her escape and so that they could fight their way off the [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]]. After Ulgo and Revan battled through many [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith troopers]] and discovered that Shan had already evacuated the ship, they eventually came across a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] named [[Darth Bandon]]. Knowing that the Sith could [[Death/Legends|kill]] both of them, Ulgo sacrificed himself by holding off Bandon so that Revan could escape the doomed [[Warship/Legends|warship]]. Ulgo's sacrifice allowed Revan to leave the ship in the last remaining [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] along with a Republic [[Officer/Legends|officer]] named [[Carth Onasi]].

==Biography==
===Serving the Republic===
{{Quote|I'm your bunkmate here on the ''Endar Spire''. We work opposite shifts, I guess that's why you haven't seen me before.|Trask Ulgo, introducing himself to Revan|audio=TraskUlgo-Bunkmate.flac|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] named Trask Ulgo<ref name="KotOR" /> was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] into [[House Ulgo]], an [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Noble/Legends|noble]] family<ref name="PaymentDue" /> that was embroiled in a rivalry with [[House of Organa/Legends|House Organa]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. Eventually,<ref name="KotOR" /> he<ref name="TCSWE" /> joined the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Navy]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and had attained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Ensign/Legends|ensign]] by the height of the [[Jedi Civil War]], which was fought between the Republic and the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]], led by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak]], two [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]–turned–[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lords]].<ref name="KotOR" /> By [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="KotORDate" /> Ulgo served as an [[Republic officer/Legends|officer]] aboard the [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]] ''[[Endar Spire]]''. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], he was assigned to the same quarters as a Republic recruit who was actually the [[Amnesia/Legends|amnesiac]] former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] Revan, although Ulgo was unaware of his bunkmate's identity. Ulgo and Revan worked opposite shifts while onboard the [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], and they did not have opportunities to interact with each other. During the [[Time/Legends|time]] Revan and Ulgo were bunkmates, the ''Endar Spire'' was placed under the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of [[Bastila Shan]], a Jedi whose skill in [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] was essential to the Republic's war effort.<ref name="KotOR" />

Under Shan's command, the ''Endar Spire'' was [[Attack on the Endar Spire|ambushed]] by a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] commanded by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Malak in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] over the planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. While the rest of the cruiser's crew and complement of Republic [[Republic soldier|troops]] attempted to fend off the boarding parties of [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith troopers]] and [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]], Ulgo returned to his quarters within the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] command module to obtain his bunkmate's aid in protecting Shan and escaping the ship. Before Ulgo arrived, Revan was roused into consciousness by the battle and the ''Endar Spire''{{'s}} shuddering as it was wracked by [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] fire from [[Sith fighter|Sith interceptors]]. Ulgo explained the situation to Revan and stated that it was their duty to ensure that Shan evacuated the ship. After collecting Revan's gear, they fought their way toward the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] through several groups of Sith forces in the ship's corridors, until they stumbled upon a short [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|duel]] between a [[Unidentified Jedi Master (Endar Spire)|Jedi]] and a [[Unidentified Dark Jedi (Endar Spire)|Dark Jedi]]. Although the Jedi was victorious, she was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by an explosion shortly after defeating the Dark Jedi, leaving Ulgo and Revan to continue on to the bridge.<ref name="KotOR" />

[[File:DamnAnotherDarkJedi-KOTOR.jpg|thumb|left|210px|Trask Ulgo engages Darth Bandon.]]
The pair eventually arrived at the bridge and defeated the Sith [[Troop/Legends|troops]] holding the position, although they found that Shan had already evacuated and was headed to the ship's [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]]. Reasoning that the Sith would destroy the ship once Shan escaped, Ulgo decided that their only option was to flee to the planet as well. However, before Ulgo and Revan could make it to the pods located on the ship's starboard side, Ulgo heard a noise behind a door and opened it, revealing Malak's [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Darth Bandon]]. Upon seeing the Sith, Ulgo realized that there was little chance that they could survive a battle with the [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]]-wielding Sith Lord. In order to save Revan, he charged into battle with Bandon and closed the door behind him to ensure that he could buy his comrade enough time to escape the ship.<ref name="KotOR" /> Bandon ultimately overcame and killed Ulgo,<ref name="TCSWE" /> although Revan and another Republic [[Officer/Legends|officer]] named [[Carth Onasi]] used the last escape pod to evacuate the ship before it was destroyed.<ref name="KotOR" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Centuries ago, Trask Ulgo bravely sacrificed himself in combat with Darth Bandon. Trask's martyrdom saved the Galactic Republic.|Peema Ahuff, curator of the Royal Museum of Alderaan|audio=HoorayForRevansHumanShield-TOR.flac|<ref name="Star Wars: The Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
Later that year,<ref name="KotORDate" /> after having been [[Jedi training/Legends|retrained]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and sent out by the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] in search of the a Sith-controlled device known as the [[Star Forge]], Revan encountered Darth Bandon once again,<ref name="KotOR" /> in the [[Water/Legends|underwater]] [[Hrakert Station]] on the planet [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]].<ref name="Manaan">In ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', the player can fight [[Darth Bandon]] on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]], or [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Due to the ordering of the storyline presented in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'', this article assumes that Revan defeats Bandon on Manaan.</ref> Along with his companions, Revan bested Malak's apprentice and his two Dark Jedi followers in a [[Mission to Hrakert Station|duel]], which resulted in the death of Ulgo's killer. Ultimately, Trask Ulgo's sacrifice on the ''Endar Spire'' allowed Revan to [[Battle of Rakata Prime|defeat]] Malak and the Sith Empire, which Revan had once controlled,<ref name="KotOR" /> as well as hold off invasion from another [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]] for almost three [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]].<ref name="Revan">''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' established that [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] was captured by the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]] in [[3951 BBY]], at which point Revan began influencing him to prevent the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] from attacking the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Additionally, ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]'' stated that the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]] [[Great Galactic War|invaded]] the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in [[3681 BBY]]. Combining these two dates results in a [[Time/Legends|time]] period of 270 [[Standard year/Legends|years]].</ref>

Ulgo's sacrifice in confronting Bandon was admired centuries after his death, and his family became quite powerful on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Alderaan. [[Bouris Ulgo]], the self-proclaimed [[King/Legends|King]] of Alderaan and head of House Ulgo during the [[Alderaan Civil War]], revered Trask Ulgo greatly. [[Peema Ahuff]], the curator of the [[Royal Museum of Alderaan]], believed that Ulgo's sacrifice was an act of martyrdom and that he remained one of Alderaan's noblest sons. Ahuff also linked Ulgo's sacrifice to Revan's defeat of Malak.<ref name="PaymentDue" />

{{GameLSmechanics}}
In [[3643 BBY]],<ref name="TORDate">Act I of the [[Voidhound|smuggler]] storyline in ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' takes place in [[3643 BBY]] per the reasoning [[Template:TOR dates|here]].</ref> Peema Ahuff acquired the preserved head of Darth Bandon from a [[Voidhound|smuggler]] with the intentions of presenting the head to Bouris Ulgo as a peace offering in order to improve relations between Ahuff's [[House Alde]] and the king.<ref name="PaymentDue" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|For the Republic!|Trask Ulgo|audio=TraskUlgo-FortheRepublic.flac|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
[[File:Trask.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Trask Ulgo served in the Republic Navy.]]
During his time serving in the Republic Navy, Trask Ulgo was dedicated to the Republic cause and took his duty seriously. Due to his sense of duty, Ulgo was outraged by cowardice and disloyalty. Despite the fact that the odds were against the Republic forces on the ''Endar Spire'', he still believed that he should locate and aid his Jedi commander rather than simply evacuate the ship. Ulgo also held the combat skills of both the Jedi and the Sith in high regard, believing that common [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] would merely impede combat fought between Jedi and Sith. When he and Revan encountered Darth Bandon on the ''Endar Spire'', Ulgo stayed behind to hold off the Sith and buy his companion the time necessary to escape the dying ship. Ulgo had fair skin, [[Color/Legends|blond]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], and blue [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="KotOR" />

==Skills and abilities==
{{Quote|Your melee weapon is made using a cortosis weave. It's strong enough to stand up against anything, even a lightsaber.|Trask Ulgo, to Revan|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
Trask Ulgo was trained in the use of various types of [[Armor/Legends|armor]] as well as in the use of [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapons]], [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]], [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]], and heavy weaponry. Ulgo also had knowledge of close-quarters combat tactics and the [[cortosis-weave]] design used in the Republic's melee weapons to make them resistant to [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] strikes. He had some training in first-aid and in overriding the security systems present in doors and storage containers.<ref name="KotOR" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|Good thing we have medpacs to heal our wounds. It might be a good idea to use one now before our next battle.|Trask Ulgo, to Revan|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
While on the ''Endar Spire'', Trask Ulgo wore a standard suit of Republic armor and carried a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] pistol. When the Sith forces attacked the ship, Ulgo also had access to a [[Medpac/Legends|medpac]] and a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]].<ref name="KotOR" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Because of the attack this room is in lockdown, but don't worry—I've got the override codes. You'll have to use me to unlock the door.|Trask Ulgo explains switching between characters to the player|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
Trask Ulgo first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', a [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] produced by [[BioWare]]. While every other party member with understandable speech in the game has a credited voice actor, Ulgo's voice actor was not identified in the game's credits. Ulgo serves as the player's first party member, with much of his dialogue serving as a tutorial to show the player how to use game controls. After the ''Endar Spire'' portion of the game, he is removed from the party selection screen, with his portrait later being filled by Bastila Shan. If the player removes Ulgo's standard [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] in the equipment screen, his appearance will not change, although it will change normally if his clothing is replaced with a different set of armor.<ref name="KotOR" />

When the player confronts Darth Bandon later on in the game, the player may choose to accuse Bandon of killing Ulgo, although Bandon does not confirm that he killed Ulgo.<ref name="KotOR" /> Ulgo's death was ultimately confirmed in Darth Bandon's entry in the [[2008]] ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'', published for [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="KotORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', also released in 2008, misidentified Ulgo as a [[Corporal/Legends|corporal]] first class.<ref name="TCSWE" />

In ''Knights of the Old Republic'', the player could encounter [[Selven]], a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who killed all the members of the Ulgo family on Taris and who was rumored to have been hired by the Organa family. However, the game did not explicitly connect the Ulgo family to Trask Ulgo.<ref name="KotOR" /> In [[2010]], an article for Bouris Ulgo released through [[The Holonet]], an online database with information relating to the ''Knights of the Old Republic'' MMORPG sequel, ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', hinted at the relationship between House Ulgo and Trask Ulgo in [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]] text that translated to "for Trask."<ref name="Bouris">{{HolonetOld|biographies|bouris-ulgo|Bouris Ulgo}}</ref> When ''Star Wars: The Old Republic'' was released in [[2011]], the relationship between House Ulgo and Trask Ulgo was confirmed in "Payment Due," a bonus quest for members of the [[Smuggler/Legends|Smuggler]] class.<ref name="PaymentDue" />
